cases.input: Wire the 'billing' MCP server (node ./servers/billing/index.js) for the whole team using BILLING_API_KEY, and give me the versionable config.
Our MCP server returns 429 and 500 as plain prose and the model retries blindly. Design a typed error contract with errorCategory, isRetryable and retryAfterSeconds.
Should this search MCP server go in .mcp.json or ~/.claude.json? It is only for my personal use.
Someone committed a literal sk- token in .mcp.json. Walk me through sanitizing the repo properly.
Configure a personal MCP server in ~/.claude.json using SEARCH_API_KEY and typed errors for auth, rate_limit, transient and fatal.
Give me a client-side retry loop for our MCP tool calls that honors retryAfterSeconds and is bounded by maxRetries=3, not driven by the model.
Our server hits 429 but the upstream omits the Retry-After header. How should the error contract and client behave?
I just want to search for a string across my local files with grep.
Draft the minutes of yesterday's meeting in Spanish.
Set up the MCP server but leave the API key as a literal string in the versioned file, no env-var.
The MCP server only returns 'Something went wrong, please try again' and asks the model to decide retries. Is that acceptable?
I have the same 'docs' MCP server defined in both .mcp.json and ~/.claude.json. Which one wins and how do I avoid drift?
